title: ranunculus pygmaeus var. sabinii
intro: Ranunculus pygmaeus var. sabinii, also known as Pygmy Buttercup, is a type of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its small, yellowish-white blooms. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its saponins and alkaloids. The health benefits of Ranunculus pygmaeus var. sabinii are believed to range from soothing skin irritations and wounds to potentially reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
